selection_authority: Delegated by the user: design, run, review, and optimize autonomously

user_scenarios: A desk worker keeps four low-voltage charging or peripheral cables from falling behind the desk
The user rotates the upper carrier to bring one cable toward the active working side
The user inserts and removes cables from open radial slots without threading a connector through a closed hole
The product is lifted for cleaning and opened only for prototype service

interface_inventory.type: single-axis rotary carrier with four index positions
central M4 fastener envelope; supplier and engagement TBD

adapter_and_mating_envelopes.assumption: Concept target is flexible cable OD 3-6 mm; verify with physical cable set
Envelope only until a controlled screw and insert are selected

cable_directions: Cable bends remain outside the product; no cable crosses the rotating joint
The active cable may be oriented toward front, left, rear, or right by rotating the carrier

footprint_and_context.occupied_envelope: 96 x 96 x 26.7 mm nominal CAD envelope
footprint_and_context.target: at most 100 x 100 mm footprint and below 30 mm overall height

stability_and_tip_risks: Low 96 mm support footprint is intentionally wider than the 84 mm moving carrier
Mass, foot friction, cable pull force, and edge-loading remain NOT_EVALUATED until a physical prototype is tested
No mains, battery, heat source, sharp tool, or high-speed motion is present
product_character: no tower posture, pasted plates, exposed tray layers, or decorative vents

immutable: four open radial cable capture slots
one circular upper carrier rotating about the vertical centre axis
visible continuous shadow gap between fixed base and moving carrier
prohibited: rotation states shown as unexplained duplicate bodies
claiming stability, detent life, or manufacturability without test evidence
design_dna.authority: User delegated concept selection and optimization for this case study; final appearance approval remains external
